mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically increasing present valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the rising unemployment signal, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in residential housing starts and consumer credit outstanding indicate a synchronized contraction in household balance sheets and spending capacity, which compresses corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy in walk-forward testing, or forward returns show no statistically significant difference between triggered and non-triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding capacity utilization and robust aggregate supply, which supports corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns in high-industrial-production regimes are not significantly higher than low-production regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth reflects strengthening aggregate demand, which directly lifts corporate revenue trajectories and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as market participants price in higher near-term earnings.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of observations, or the predictive correlation between GDP growth acceleration and SPX returns falls below 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.0 percent, the cost of capital rises sufficiently to compress equity valuations, particularly for duration-sensitive growth stocks, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.0%, or the signal shows no directional edge compared to a random baseline over a 5-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising nonfarm payrolls and a stable unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, which sustains consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings momentum accelerates.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to add explanatory power beyond single-series models, or forward returns in confirmed labor expansion periods do not significantly exceed baseline returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in housing starts acts as a leading indicator for broader construction activity and household wealth effects, which lifts consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The housing starts signal produces negative forward returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for concurrent GDP growth changes.
